title: Object Management Toolbar on ERD Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user working on the ERD page, I want to add and manage data model objects directly from the ERD without switching to the Dictionary page, so that I can build my ERD efficiently in one place.
description:
Replicates the object management toolbar from the Dictionary page onto the ERD page. Instead of a simple '+' button, the ERD page now uses the same button and toolbar experience as the Dictionary page for opening, adding, and hiding the object management controls.

This ensures a consistent user experience across tabs — as users switch between Dictionary and ERD, they encounter the same interaction patterns for managing objects. Based on revisions from MBT-763.
acceptance_criteria: The ERD page includes the same object management toolbar as the Dictionary page
Users can add objects to the data model directly from the ERD page
The toolbar open/close behavior matches the Dictionary page experience
The previous '+' button is replaced with the replicated toolbar button
Object additions from the ERD page are immediately reflected in the diagram
ux_changes: Replicated the Dictionary page object management toolbar on the ERD page, replacing the previous '+' button. Users get an identical experience for adding and managing objects across both pages.
middleware_changes: Shared the Dictionary page's object management controller logic on the ERD page, reusing existing code for the toolbar and object add operations.
implementation_notes: Based on the revised UX from MBT-763. The same button component and toolbar behavior from the Dictionary page was ported to the ERD page for consistency.
comment_summary: Developer completed the implementation and merged to staging for review. Straightforward port of existing Dictionary toolbar to the ERD page.
